## Conclusion
- In this offline pack, 中国海油 is the cleanest direct beneficiary, 招商轮船 is a conditional second-order beneficiary, 中海油服 is lagged and capex-dependent, and 卫星化学 is the most likely cost-taker.

## Confirmed
- The pack confirms a Middle East shock headline and confirms that physical disruption is still separate from risk premium in the current evidence layer.
- 中国海油 is an upstream resource owner with direct crude sensitivity, so it is the clearest first-round beneficiary in the comparison set.
- 招商轮船 only becomes a stronger beneficiary if freight tightness or rerouting persists, while 中海油服 needs order-cycle transmission rather than same-day spot-beta logic.
- 卫星化学 sits downstream and should be tested for margin squeeze before calling it a beneficiary.

## Unconfirmed
- The pack does not confirm a prolonged physical outage, so tanker and service upside remains conditional rather than fully confirmed.
- The durability of any freight-rate spike and the capex read-through into 中海油服 are still likely rather than fully confirmed.

## Risks
- Treating headline price action as proof of earnings transmission would overstate the case for service and shipping names.
- Collapsing physical disruption and risk premium into one bucket would make the beneficiary map look more certain than the evidence supports.

## Next Step
- Refresh the live event tape and benchmark board, then rerun the chain map with exact dates for Brent, freight proxies, and any confirmed physical disruption signals.
